answer:
Honestly, when I think about chances I wish I’d taken, one that really stands out is joining the school debate team back in my sophomore year. At the time, I was kind of intimidated by the idea of public speaking, and I thought maybe I wouldn’t be good enough, you know? But looking back now, I realize that stepping out of my comfort zone could’ve made a huge difference for me. If I had joined, I probably would’ve developed much stronger communication skills and maybe even boosted my confidence when speaking in front of others. Also, debate teaches you how to think critically and defend your ideas — which are super useful skills, especially now that I’m applying to universities in the US.

I mean, taking that chance might have helped me network with people who share similar interests, and perhaps even opened doors to more leadership opportunities in school. For example, some of my friends who did debate ended up participating in regional competitions and got letters of recommendation from their coaches, which is honestly pretty impressive. Sometimes I wonder if not taking that chance means I missed out on experiences that would have helped me become more articulate and resilient.

But, at the same time, I guess it taught me something about myself — like how fear can sometimes hold me back. Now, as I’m preparing for this next stage of my life, I try to remind myself to embrace new opportunities, even if they seem daunting. So, yeah, I really believe that if I had joined the debate team, things could have been quite different, but now I’m motivated to take more risks moving forward.
title: What is a chance that you wish you had taken, and how might things be different if you have taken that chance?
